key_points: The production chain of a shirt demonstrates how different sectors of the economy are interconnected
Garment production often involves subcontracting where small factories work for large companies
Workers at the lower levels of the production chain receive minimal wages despite contributing significantly
Export companies and multinational brands dominate the global clothing market
Market competition and demand affect pricing, wages, and production decisions
examples: Small stitching units produce garments for export companies
Large brands outsource production to reduce costs
Retail stores sell branded shirts at prices much higher than production costs
Garment workers work long hours but earn limited wages
Global fashion trends influence clothing production in many countries
formulas: Subcontracting → Hiring smaller units or factories to complete part of production
Global Market → Trade and sale of goods across different countries
Supply Chain → Network of individuals and organizations involved in production and distribution
exam_tips: Focus on understanding economic inequality in the production chain
Remember the concept of subcontracting and its role in garment industries
Use examples to explain how globalization affects clothing markets
Practice explaining the relationship between workers, companies, and consumers
